name: Jason Yates
subject: Two Principles We Left Behind
body:
| | Words from Martin Luther King Jr.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iT64WnftY5I-Pk4AtGN-B0x48m3Q9aP08J6NY78jQNmamuxza3vd5CjJiU1sohH8sA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190121com12&n=1)
| Peter, For over 50 years we have set aside a day each January to remember Rev. Martin Luther King Jr., best known for his leadership in the civil rights movement. Less known is the fact that he was a pastor whose life was guided by the Word of God. Here’s an excerpt from a sermon he delivered in 1954 to Ebenezer Baptist Church in Detroit Michigan. His words still ring true and are relevant for our culture today. _“There is something wrong with our world, something fundamentally and basically wrong. I don’t think we have to look too far to see that. I’m sure that most of you would agree with me in making that assertion. And when we stop to analyze the cause of our world’s ills, many things come to mind._ _We begin to wonder if it is due to the fact that we don’t know enough. But it can’t be that. Because in terms of accumulated knowledge we know more today than men have known in any period of human history...”_ **He went on to provide the answer to man’s problems.** _“I think we have to look much deeper than that if we are to find the real cause of man’s problems and the real cause of the world’s ills today. If we are to really find it I think we will have to look in the hearts and souls of men._ _The trouble isn’t so much that we don’t know enough, but it’s as if we aren’t good enough. The trouble isn’t so much that our scientific genius lags behind, but our moral genius lags behind. The great problem facing modern man is that, that the means by which we live, have outdistanced the spiritual ends for which we live. So we find ourselves caught in a messed-up world. The problem is with man himself and man’s soul..._ _My friends, all I’m trying to say is that if we are to go forward today, we’ve got to go back and rediscover some mighty precious values that we’ve left behind. That’s the only way that we would be able to make of our world a better world, and to make of this world what God wants it to be and the real purpose and meaning of it. The only way we can do it is to go back, and rediscover some mighty precious values that we’ve left behind.”_ **What are those precious values Rev. King believed our nation had left behind?** _1\. “The first is this—the first principle of value that we need to rediscover is this—that**all reality hinges on moral foundations**. In other words, that this is a moral universe, and that there are moral laws of the universe, just as abiding as the physical laws...”_ _2\. “Then there is a second thing, a second principle that we’ve got to go back and rediscover. And that is that all reality has spiritual control. In other words, we’ve got to go back and rediscover the principle that**there is a God behind the process...** ”_ As we remember Rev. King’s legacy today, I encourage you to take some time to [listen to or read his sermon “Rediscovering Lost Values”](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iT64WnftY5I-Pk4AtGN-B0x48m3Q9aP08J6NY78jQNmamuxza3vd5CjJiU1sohH8sA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190121com12&n=2) in its entirety. You will be inspired to carry on the timeless biblical values that our nation is so prone to forget. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iT64WnftY5I-Pk4AtGN-B0x48m3Q9aP08J6NY78jQNmamuxza3vd5CjJiU1sohH8sA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190121com12&n=3) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190121com12&n=4)
---

name: West Virginia Democratic Party
subject: Today we honor Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
Fellow Democrat,
Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was a true champion for justice and equality in America. Dr. King fought and inspired others to fight against discrimination of all forms, but most of all discrimination in our justice system.
Because of Dr. King's fight for civil rights we have made great progress in America, but sadly we find ourselves feeling as if we are sliding backwards in our efforts to push forward. We must continue to ask ourselves what we can do to end prejudice in our justice system and our communities.
What can we do to help those who are denied equal opportunity still today? What can we do to push down the barriers created by prejudice? What can we do to end the attacks on our fellow Americans because of the color of their skin and what can we do to end discrimination of all forms?
Today we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. by continuing the fight. Today we urge you to ask how can help and encourage you to donate to local organizations who fight to end inequality in our society and our justice system.
Sincerely,
_Belinda Biafore, Chairwoman_
_Team WVDP_

Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www.wvdems.org). This communication is not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Public employees may disregard. Contributions are not tax-deductible.
West Virginia Democratic Party
PO Box 11926
Charleston , WV 25339
United States

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: A lesson from Dr. King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | | | _"So often we overlook the work and the significance of those who are not in professional jobs, of those who are not in the so-called 'big jobs.'**But let me say to you tonight, that whenever you are engaged in work that serves humanity and is for the building of humanity, it has dignity, and it has worth."**_ \-- Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., _March 18, 1968_
---
Today we remember someone who taught our nation a multitude of important lessons on justice, fairness, courage and equality -- Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
One of those lessons he summed up with four simple words that are as true now as the day he spoke them: **"All labor has dignity."**
Whether you go to work in a suit or an apron, a uniform or jeans: Your work has dignity. We take pride in the things we create, in the services we render, in the jobs we do for ourselves and for each other.
Yet over the past few decades, people have been forced to work harder and longer for less pay and fewer benefits. For the past 31 days, hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been sent home, or forced to work without pay, due to the government shutdown.
In this nation, in the America I want for myself and my children and grandchildren, work would always be respected and rewarded. Dr. King taught us this lesson all those years ago -- and it's one our leaders should take to heart today.
With gratitude,
Sherrod

|
---

name: Tim Kaine
subject: "For here on either side of the wall are God's children and no man-made barrier can obliterate that fact"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek:

If you turn on the TV right now, you're more likely to hear the words "wall" or "shutdown" than "justice" or "dream." On a day when we ought to be reflecting about the dreams we have, the dreams Dr. King had, we're instead talking about the 31st day of a government shutdown and the petulance and hatred that is forcing it.

So right now, let us take a minute to look back at some words of Dr. King's that are particularly resonant today. He spoke truth to so many situations that we still reckon with, and here's one piece of wisdom that I think acknowledges where we stand in his continuing legacy.

In September of 1964, Dr. King spoke near the Berlin Wall. A wall that was built smack in the center of Berlin, separating west from east to keep people divided from one another. On that day, this is what he said:
> _It is indeed an honor to be in this city, which stands as a symbol of the divisions of men on the face of the earth. For here on either side of the wall are God 's children and no man-made barrier can obliterate that fact. Whether it be East or West, men and women search for meaning, hope for fulfillment, yearn for faith in something beyond themselves, and cry desperately for love and community to support them in this pilgrim journey. Wherever reconciliation is taking place, wherever people are 'breaking down the dividing walls of hostility' which separate them from their brothers, there Christ continues to perform his ministry of reconciliation._
**Spoken 55 years ago, but still as meaningful as the day Dr. King said those words. While he is no longer with us, his legacy, his example, and his wisdom continue to this very day.**

Today marks a celebration of the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. And as we consider his thoughts and his dreams, the question is not what we think of his story, but rather where we find ourselves in his story -- and how we can be a part of his continuing legacy.

_-Tim_
---
* * *
|
---
[](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f175b/3e0906/7a6c2ea6/3e3751fe/3931673391/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/timkaine?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=20190121_1_NA_TK_MLK&source=20190121_1_NA_TK_MLK") [](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f175b/3e0906/7a6c2ea6/3e3751fd/3931673391/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/timkaine?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=20190121_1_NA_TK_MLK&source=20190121_1_NA_TK_MLK") [](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f175b/3e0906/7a6c2ea6/3e3751fc/3931673391/VEsF/ "https://www.instagram.com/timkaine/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=20190121_1_NA_TK_MLK&source=20190121_1_NA_TK_MLK")
